verses.1: For the music director; on Muth-labben. A Psalm of David. I will give thanks to the LORD with all my heart; I will tell of all Your wonders.
verses.2: I will rejoice and be jubilant in You; I will sing praise to Your name, O Most High.
verses.3: When my enemies turn back, They stumble and perish before You.
verses.4: For You have maintained my just cause; You have sat on the throne judging righteously.
verses.5: You have rebuked the nations, You have eliminated the wicked; You have wiped out their name forever and ever.
verses.6: The enemy has come to an end in everlasting ruins, And You have uprooted the cities; The very memory of them has perished.
verses.7: But the LORD sits as King forever; He has established His throne for judgment,
verses.8: And He will judge the world in righteousness; He will execute judgment for the peoples fairly.
verses.9: The LORD will also be a stronghold for the oppressed, A stronghold in times of trouble;
verses.10: And those who know Your name will put their trust in You, For You, LORD, have not abandoned those who seek You.
verses.11: Sing praises to the LORD, who dwells in Zion; Declare His deeds among the peoples.
verses.12: For He who requires blood remembers them; He does not forget the cry of the needy.
verses.13: Be gracious to me, LORD; See my oppression from those who hate me, You who lift me up from the gates of death,
verses.14: So that I may tell of all Your praises, That in the gates of the daughter of Zion I may rejoice in Your salvation.
verses.15: The nations have sunk down into the pit which they have made; In the net which they hid, their own foot has been caught.
verses.16: The LORD has made Himself known; He has executed judgment. A wicked one is ensnared in the work of his own hands. Higgaion Selah
verses.17: The wicked will return to Sheol, All the nations who forget God.
verses.18: For the needy will not always be forgotten, Nor the hope of the afflicted perish forever.
verses.19: Arise, LORD, do not let mankind prevail; Let the nations be judged before You.
verses.20: Put them in fear, LORD; Let the nations know that they are merely human. Selah
